title: Your Carbon Footprint Calculator
description: Calculate your personal carbon footprint across energy, transport, food, and consumption — then discover exactly where to cut.

stages.title: Your Lifestyle Has a Carbon Price Tag
stages.body:
Every choice you make — what you eat, how you travel, what you buy — has a carbon cost. The average American generates **~16 tons of CO₂ per year**. The global target to stay below 1.5°C? **~2 tons per person by 2030**.

That's an **87% reduction** needed. Scary? Or empowering — because once you know where your emissions come from, you can act precisely.

Let's calculate your footprint right now.

stages.title: Your Carbon Inputs
stages.description: Adjust your lifestyle factors to calculate your personal carbon footprint.
stages.resultTemplate:
Estimated annual footprint: {{result}} tons CO₂/year
US avg: 16 tons · Global avg: 4.7 tons · Target: 2 tons

questions.question: What's the carbon footprint of a round-trip transatlantic flight?
questions.explanation: A round-trip economy transatlantic flight generates approximately 1.6 tons CO₂ per passenger.

questions.question: Which food has the highest carbon footprint per kg?
questions.explanation: Beef produces ~27 kg CO₂-eq per kg of food — roughly 10x chicken and 50x vegetables.

questions.question: What single home change saves the most CO₂?
questions.explanation: Rooftop solar can offset 3-8 tons CO₂/year depending on location and system size.

questions.question: How much could plant-rich diets reduce food emissions?
questions.explanation: Shifting toward plant-rich diets could reduce food system emissions by up to 50%.

stages.title: Your Journey Through Climate Knowledge is Complete
stages.body:
You've journeyed from a single carbon atom through the Keeling Curve, carbon budgets, restoration projects, carbon markets, drawdown solutions, and your own footprint.

**What you now know:**
- The science behind atmospheric CO₂ rise
- Earth's carbon budget and how close we are to exhausting it
- Real solutions that exist right now
- How markets can (and can't) help solve this crisis
- Your personal impact and where to focus

**What you can do next:**
- Share this knowledge — understanding is the first step
- Visit your **Pledge vs. Reality globe** to see your country's commitments
- Support verified carbon projects
- Advocate for systemic change in your community

*The Earth doesn't need saving. It will persist. What needs saving is the climate that civilization depends on.*
